DocGuard AI Workflow Guide

Header Block

Confidence Summary: No procedural steps in this section; product identity and scope are verified from the repository documentation.

FieldValue
Product nameDocGuard AI
Product typeSaaS/software, delivered as a local-first Windows desktop and command-line application
Product versionv1.2.0 RC1 U6.1
Guide date2026-06-24
Primary end-user audienceDocument owners, reviewers, analysts, records teams, and operators who inspect, clean, verify, and report on files
Primary administrator audienceIT administrators, release managers, security reviewers, support leads, licensing administrators, and data governance owners
Environment notesWindows 10 or Windows 11 endpoints; portable package, installer package, or source validation; local processing for document workflows; optional TSG License Manager for licensing, entitlement refresh, deactivation, and update checks
PurposeProvide one operational workflow guide that separates day-to-day DocGuard AI use from administrative deployment, governance, licensing, and evidence-retention responsibilities.

Grounding corpus used: README.md, docs/USER_GUIDE.md, docs/ADMIN_GUIDE.md, docs/QUICK_START.md, docs/INSTALL_AND_RUN.md, docs/CLI_REFERENCE.md, docs/LICENSING.md, docs/TSG_PLATFORM_INTEGRATION.md, profiles/standard.json, profiles/aggressive.json, profiles/paranoid.json, and implementation checks in sanitize/cli.py, sanitize/automation.py, sanitize/engine.py, sanitize/backup.py, and sanitize/history.py.

At-a-Glance

Confidence Summary: No procedural steps in this section; capability statements are verified from the repository documentation.

Capability AreaEnd User Can DoAdministrator Can Do
Source selectionSelect one file or a folder for review.Define approved source locations and endpoint access expectations.
ScanInspect supported files before changing them.Run CLI scans, diagnose discovery issues, and generate scan reports.
CleanClean supported files after reviewing findings and safety controls.Set profile policy, restrict unsafe options, and validate post-clean outcomes.
CleanroomRun the full scan, clean, post-scan, verify, report, and Activity workflow.Automate Cleanroom with CLI, batch jobs, watch mode, and scheduled-task helpers.
ReportsReview local HTML reports and retain them with the relevant release or case record.Configure report locations, retention expectations, and evidence handling.
ActivityConfirm recent workflow results from the Activity page.Query history.db for recent runs, stats, and top-risk runs.
LicensingView license status when support or an administrator asks.Activate, refresh, deactivate, and update-check through local or TSG License Manager paths.
ExtensionsView enabled core Extensions and integration/module packs.Verify expected Extension status and bridge commands for integrations.

Setup / Onboarding Sequence

Confidence Summary: Procedural steps in this section are 100% [verified], 0% inferred, and 0% unknown.

End-User Track

Precondition: DocGuard AI is installed, extracted as a portable package, or made available by an administrator; the user has access to the files to be reviewed.

  1. Confirm you have permission to inspect and clean the target content before selecting it in DocGuard AI. [verified]
  2. Launch DocGuardAI.exe from the Start menu, desktop shortcut, or portable folder. [verified]
  3. Confirm the window title shows DocGuard AI v1.2.0 RC1 U6.1. [verified]
  4. Confirm the left sidebar is visible with Dashboard, File Review, Findings, Writing Assistant, Risk Map, Cleanroom, Reports, Activity, Extensions, and Settings. [verified]
  5. Click Browse and select a source file or folder. [verified]
  6. Confirm the selected path appears in the Source field. [verified]
  7. Leave Recursive enabled when the selected folder's subfolders should be included. [verified]
  8. Clear Recursive when only the selected folder level should be included. [verified]
  9. Select a profile, with aggressive as the recommended first choice for release preparation. [verified]
  10. Keep Backup enabled unless an administrator gives a different recovery control. [verified]
  11. Turn Dry Run on for the first review of critical or unfamiliar files. [verified]
  12. Click Scan to inspect the source before cleaning. [verified]

Expected result: DocGuard AI populates Dashboard metrics, File Review, Findings, Risk Map, and Recent Activity for the selected source.

Confirm success: The Dashboard shows files scanned, total findings, highest risk, and recent activity for the selected source.

Admin Track

Precondition: The administrator has the approved package or source workspace and a target endpoint or validation environment.

  1. Use release\DocGuardAISetup.exe for the approved installer path. [verified]
  2. Use dist-docguard-ui-polish\DocGuardAI\DocGuardAI.exe for approved portable validation or controlled file-share deployment. [verified]
  3. Confirm users have read and execute access to the installed or extracted application folder. [verified]
  4. Confirm users have write access to the intended report and Activity location. [verified]
  5. Launch DocGuard AI on the endpoint. [verified]
  6. Confirm the application title is DocGuard AI v1.2.0 RC1 U6.1. [verified]
  7. Confirm the full sidebar is visible: Dashboard, File Review, Findings, Writing Assistant, Risk Map, Cleanroom, Reports, Activity, Extensions, and Settings. [verified]
  8. Run a scan against a known safe sample. [verified]
  9. Confirm Reports and Activity populate after a Cleanroom run. [verified]
  10. For source-based validation only, create a virtual environment, install with pip install -e ., and launch with sanitize-gui, py main.py, or py -m sanitize. [verified]

Expected result: The endpoint can launch DocGuard AI, run a known-safe sample scan, and produce Reports and Activity evidence.

Confirm success: The application opens, expected navigation is present, a sample scan completes, and an Activity row is recorded.

Core Operating Procedures

Confidence Summary: Procedural steps in this section are 100% [verified], 0% inferred, and 0% unknown.

Task 1: Inspect A Source With Scan

Trigger: A user or administrator needs to inspect files before making changes.

End-User Track

Precondition: A file or folder source is selected in the Source field.

  1. Confirm the selected source path is correct and that you have permission to inspect the content. [verified]
  2. Select standard, aggressive, or paranoid from the Profile selector. [verified]
  3. Set Backup, Recursive, Dry Run, Typography, and Remove EXIF controls for the review. [verified]
  4. Use Dry Run for preview when working with critical or unfamiliar files. [verified]
  5. Click Scan. [verified]
  6. Wait for progress to complete. [verified]
  7. Review the Dashboard metrics. [verified]
  8. Open File Review and Findings for details. [verified]

Expected result: DocGuard AI scans supported files and populates Dashboard cards, risk distribution, top risk files, File Review, Findings, Risk Map, and Recent Activity.

Confirm success: The Dashboard shows the latest run summary and Findings lists file path, category, severity, score, finding name, and remediation.

Admin Track

Precondition: The CLI is installed or the administrator is running from the source environment.

  1. Run sanitize scan "C:\Path\To\FileOrFolder" --profile aggressive --summary for an aggregate scan summary. [verified]
  2. Add --html-report auto when an HTML scan report is required. [verified]
  3. Add --json-report <path> or --csv-report <path> when machine-readable evidence is required. [verified]
  4. Use --only-risky when the output should show only files with findings or errors. [verified]
  5. Use --diagnose when file discovery or supported-extension behavior needs investigation. [verified]

Expected result: The CLI prints scan results, optionally writes reports, and records the run in the configured Activity database.

Confirm success: The command returns a scan summary and, when requested, an HTML, JSON, or CSV report exists at the specified path.

Task 2: Review Findings And Risk

Trigger: A scan or Cleanroom run has completed and findings need review.

End-User Track

Precondition: Dashboard, File Review, Findings, or Risk Map contains results from a recent run.

  1. Open File Review. [verified]
  2. Expand the file tree. [verified]
  3. Select a file to view file-level details. [verified]
  4. Select a category to view category details. [verified]
  5. Select an individual finding to view detail and remediation guidance. [verified]
  6. Open Findings. [verified]
  7. Review high-risk rows first. [verified]
  8. Review file path, category, severity, score, finding name, and remediation. [verified]
  9. Open Risk Map to prioritize files by risk band. [verified]
  10. Decide whether to clean, document residual risk, or remove the file from the release set. [verified]

Expected result: The user understands which files and categories require cleaning, manual remediation, or residual-risk documentation.

Confirm success: High-risk files are identified and the next action for each is clear.

Admin Track

Precondition: Scan, clean, or pipeline reports are available locally.

  1. Open the generated HTML report for detailed evidence. [verified]
  2. Run sanitize history --last 20 to review recent run records. [verified]
  3. Run sanitize history --top-risk to identify highest-risk historical runs. [verified]
  4. Treat generated reports as sensitive because they may include file paths and finding details. [verified]
  5. Store reports with the related case, release record, or approved evidence location for regulated workflows. [verified]

Expected result: Findings review is supported by report evidence and Activity records.

Confirm success: The relevant report path and Activity entry are available for support, audit, or release review.

Task 3: Clean Supported Files

Trigger: Findings have been reviewed and the operator is ready to modify supported files.

End-User Track

Precondition: A scan has been reviewed and the selected source is ready for cleaning.

  1. Confirm the source path is correct before cleaning because supported files can be modified. [verified]
  2. Confirm Backup is on. [verified]
  3. Turn off Dry Run when the run should make actual changes. [verified]
  4. Select the appropriate profile. [verified]
  5. Click Clean to clean supported files. [verified]
  6. Wait for completion. [verified]
  7. Review the Dashboard, Reports, and Activity. [verified]

Expected result: Supported files with score-bearing findings are cleaned according to the selected profile; backup files are created when backup is enabled and the run is not a dry run.

Confirm success: The clean result shows cleaned files, Reports are available if generated, and Activity records the clean run.

Admin Track

Precondition: The target path exists and the administrator has defined the profile and reporting expectations.

  1. Run sanitize clean "C:\Path\To\FileOrFolder" --profile aggressive --html-report auto for a normal CLI clean workflow. [verified]
  2. Add --summary to print aggregate results after the clean operation. [verified]
  3. Add --json-report <path> or --csv-report <path> when downstream evidence processing is required. [verified]
  4. Restrict --no-backup to workflows that already have separate recovery controls. [verified]
  5. Review the clean report and Activity entry after the command completes. [verified]

Expected result: The CLI cleans supported files, writes requested reports, and records the run.

Confirm success: Cleaned file counts, artifacts removed, report paths, and Activity records match the expected target and profile.

Task 4: Run Cleanroom End To End

Trigger: A document set is ready for release preparation or controlled evidence generation.

End-User Track

Precondition: The selected source is the intended release or review set, and the user has already considered a dry-run scan.

  1. Select a source. [verified]
  2. Select a profile. [verified]
  3. Confirm safety controls. [verified]
  4. Click Run Cleanroom when ready to run scan, clean, post-clean scan, verify, report, and Activity as one pipeline. [verified]
  5. Wait for the run to complete. [verified]
  6. Review Pipeline Verification. [verified]
  7. Open Reports. [verified]
  8. Review Activity. [verified]

Expected result: The full pipeline performs scan -> clean -> post-clean scan -> verify -> report -> Activity.

Confirm success: Pipeline Verification passes or clearly identifies residual findings that require review.

Admin Track

Precondition: The CLI target exists and report/history locations are approved.

  1. Run sanitize run "C:\Path\To\Source" --profile aggressive --report-dir "C:\Path\To\Reports" for the recommended full pipeline. [verified]
  2. Pass --history-db "C:\Path\To\Reports\history.db" when Activity should be written to a specific database. [verified]
  3. Use a batch JSON file with profile, report_dir, and sources when multiple sources must be processed. [verified]
  4. Run sanitize batch jobs.json to process the batch. [verified]
  5. Run sanitize watch "C:\Path\To\DropFolder" --interval 30 when a drop folder should be polled for changed supported candidates. [verified]
  6. Run sanitize schedule nightly --profile aggressive to write a PowerShell scheduled-task helper under reports\. [verified]
  7. Review the printed schtasks command and register it according to endpoint policy. [verified]

Expected result: Cleanroom creates pre-clean scan, clean, and post-clean scan reports, verifies residual findings, and records Activity.

Confirm success: The pipeline summary shows files scanned, files cleaned, findings before and after, verification pass/fail counts, and report paths.

Task 5: Verify Results And Handle Residual Findings

Trigger: Cleanroom or a clean workflow has completed.

End-User Track

Precondition: Pipeline Verification or post-clean review is available.

  1. Review Pipeline Verification. [verified]
  2. If verification passes, open Reports and review the post-clean scan report. [verified]
  3. If verification fails, open File Review. [verified]
  4. Review each file with residual findings. [verified]
  5. Open Reports and review the post-clean scan report. [verified]
  6. Decide whether to retry with a stronger profile, perform manual remediation, or document residual risk. [verified]
  7. Do not distribute files until verification failures are understood. [verified]

Expected result: The user either confirms cleaned output is ready for final review or identifies the residual-risk path.

Confirm success: Verification status and residual findings are understood before cleaned files are shared.

Admin Track

Precondition: Pre-clean, clean, and post-clean evidence is available.

  1. Compare the pre-clean and post-clean reports. [verified]
  2. Review the Activity entry for mode, profile, target, files scanned, files cleaned, findings, removed findings, highest risk, and report path. [verified]
  3. Run sanitize history --stats when aggregate run statistics are needed. [verified]
  4. Retain pre-clean scan report, clean report, post-clean scan report, verification result, Activity entry, manual remediation notes, and final accepted artifact hash when required for controlled release workflows. [verified]

Expected result: Verification is evidenced by reports and Activity records.

Confirm success: Evidence is sufficient for release review, support, or audit handling.

Task 6: Use The Writing Assistant

Trigger: A user needs heuristic writing analysis or rule-based rewrite assistance.

End-User Track

Precondition: DocGuard AI is open and the text to review is available.

  1. Open Writing Assistant. [verified]
  2. Paste text into the editor. [verified]
  3. Review the live analysis. [verified]
  4. Review Findings and Statistics tabs. [verified]
  5. Review sentence heatmap output. [verified]
  6. Select a rewrite style: natural, humanize, executive, technical, academic, or government. [verified]
  7. Click Rewrite. [verified]
  8. Review the suggested rewrite. [verified]
  9. Review changed words and applied changes. [verified]
  10. Use Replace Original only after reviewing the rewritten text. [verified]
  11. Use Copy to copy the rewrite. [verified]

Expected result: The user receives heuristic analysis and, when requested, a rule-based rewrite suggestion.

Confirm success: The user has reviewed the analysis or rewrite and understands that the output does not prove authorship, guarantee compliance, or replace human review.

Admin Track

Precondition: The deployed build is DocGuard AI v1.2.0 RC1 U6.1 or later.

  1. Confirm Writing Assistant appears in the sidebar. [verified]
  2. Confirm Extensions lists Writing Assistant as a Core Extension. [verified]
  3. Use sanitize analyze-text --text "Paste text here" --heatmap for CLI analysis. [verified]
  4. Use sanitize rewrite --text "Paste text here" --style executive for CLI rewrite assistance. [verified]
  5. Tell support staff that Writing Assistant is included in Core and is not separately licensed in this build. [verified]

Expected result: Writing Assistant availability and command-line operation are validated.

Confirm success: The GUI and CLI both expose analysis or rewrite behavior.

Task 7: Review Reports And Activity

Trigger: A scan, clean, or Cleanroom run has produced evidence.

End-User Track

Precondition: At least one run has completed.

  1. Open Reports. [verified]
  2. Click Refresh Reports if needed. [verified]
  3. Select a report. [verified]
  4. Review the report in the viewer. [verified]
  5. Open Activity. [verified]
  6. Click Refresh Activity. [verified]
  7. Review timestamp, mode, profile, scanned files, cleaned files, findings, removed findings, and highest risk. [verified]
  8. Treat reports as sensitive if they contain paths or finding details. [verified]

Expected result: The user can confirm what ran, what was found, what changed, and where report evidence is stored.

Confirm success: The report and Activity row match the expected source and workflow.

Admin Track

Precondition: Report and Activity paths are known or defaults are in use.

  1. Use the default reports\ directory for local reports when no alternate path is specified. [verified]
  2. Use the default reports\history.db Activity database when no alternate path is specified. [verified]
  3. Run sanitize history --last 50 to inspect recent Activity. [verified]
  4. Run sanitize history --stats to inspect aggregate Activity statistics. [verified]
  5. Run sanitize history --top-risk to inspect highest-risk runs. [verified]
  6. Apply the team's retention policy to reports, Activity data, and backup files before using DocGuard AI for regulated workflows. [verified]

Expected result: Evidence can be retrieved, reviewed, and retained according to policy.

Confirm success: Reports and Activity data are available in the expected local paths.

Admin Configuration & Governance

Confidence Summary: Procedural steps in this section are 100% [verified], 0% inferred, and 0% unknown.

End-User Track

Precondition: The user is operating inside the approved deployment and needs to confirm local settings or license state.

  1. Open Settings to view product and license details. [verified]
  2. Use Tools -> License Status when an administrator asks for activation state. [verified]
  3. Do not send full product keys in screenshots or support requests. [verified]
  4. Keep Backup enabled for normal work. [verified]
  5. Use Dry Run on unfamiliar file sets. [verified]
  6. Ask an administrator before using paranoid on important documents. [verified]
  7. Ask an administrator before cleaning regulated, controlled, or business-critical document sets outside the approved workflow. [verified]

Expected result: The user can operate within approved safety, licensing, and support boundaries.

Confirm success: The user can state the active version, license status path, profile, and safety settings used.

Admin Track

Precondition: Administrative owners have been assigned for release ownership, endpoint deployment, licensing, security review, support readiness, and data governance.

  1. Use the approved installer release\DocGuardAISetup.exe for managed endpoint installation. [verified]
  2. Use the approved portable build dist-docguard-ui-polish\DocGuardAI for controlled portable deployment. [verified]
  3. Restrict deployment folder write permissions. [verified]
  4. Confirm users have read access to target source documents and write access to selected output/report locations. [verified]
  5. Confirm product identity: product name DocGuard AI, product ID docguard_ai, product key prefix DGA, edition Core, executable DocGuardAI.exe. [verified]
  6. Run sanitize plugins to verify expected Extension status. [verified]
  7. Run sanitize license to check license status. [verified]
  8. Activate offline with sanitize license --activate DGA-XXXX-XXXX-XXXX only through an approved product-key handling path. [verified]
  9. Activate online with sanitize license --activate DGA-XXXX-XXXX-XXXX --online --server-url https://<tsg-platform-url> only against the approved TSG License Manager endpoint. [verified]
  10. Refresh online licensing with sanitize license --refresh --server-url https://<tsg-platform-url>. [verified]
  11. Deactivate locally or online with sanitize license --deactivate or sanitize license --deactivate --online --server-url https://<tsg-platform-url> when access must be removed. [verified]
  12. Set TSG_API_URL or pass --server-url for TSG License Manager operations. [verified]
  13. Run sanitize update-check --server-url https://<tsg-platform-url> when update checks are part of the environment. [verified]
  14. Define profile policy: standard for conservative cleanup, aggressive for normal release preparation, and paranoid only after sample validation for business-critical document sets. [verified]
  15. Keep Backup enabled in the GUI unless another recovery mechanism exists. [verified]
  16. Restrict CLI use of --no-backup to workflows with separate recovery controls. [verified]
  17. Use an approved reports folder for regulated workflows. [verified]
  18. Treat generated reports as sensitive because they may include file paths and finding details. [verified]
  19. Review backup retention policy before cleaning sensitive sources. [verified]
  20. Configure CLI evidence locations with --report-dir and --history-db when defaults are not appropriate. [verified]
  21. Retain pre-clean scan report, clean report, post-clean scan report, verification result, Activity entry, manual remediation notes, and final accepted artifact hash when required for controlled release workflows. [verified]
  22. Run endpoint protection scans on signed production artifacts. [verified]
  23. Review dependency vulnerability results before production release. [verified]
  24. Confirm local-only processing language in customer-facing materials. [verified]

Expected result: Deployment, license state, evidence generation, local data handling, and profile policy are governed consistently.

Confirm success: A deployment owner can show package identity, license status, plugin status, report/history locations, profile policy, and release evidence.

Lifecycle & Decision Points

Confidence Summary: No numbered procedures in this section; decision points are verified from the repository documentation and implementation behavior.

End-User Track

Decision PointTrigger ConditionBranch
Scan vs. CleanYou have not reviewed the source yet.Scan first, then decide whether cleaning is appropriate.
Dry Run vs. actual cleanThe file set is critical or unfamiliar.Use Dry Run for preview; turn it off only when ready to change supported files.
Standard vs. Aggressive vs. ParanoidThe workflow ranges from routine metadata reduction to strongest cleanup.Use standard for routine cleanup, aggressive for release preparation, and paranoid only with careful verification.
Cleanroom vs. individual actionsA final release package or controlled document set needs the complete pipeline.Use Cleanroom for scan, clean, post-clean scan, verify, report, and Activity.
Verification pass vs. failPipeline Verification reports success or residual findings.Use cleaned output only after pass; if fail, review residual findings before distribution.
Report retain vs. discardThe run supports a case, release, audit, or regulated workflow.Retain reports according to team policy and treat them as sensitive.
Manual remediation vs. retryA report says the finding is residual, unsupported, informational, or inspection-first.Follow remediation guidance, retry with a stronger profile when appropriate, or document residual risk.

Admin Track

Decision PointTrigger ConditionBranch
Installer vs. portable vs. sourceThe environment is production deployment, controlled validation, or engineering validation.Use installer for managed endpoints, portable for controlled validation/file share deployment, and source only for engineering validation.
Trial/offline vs. online TSG licensingTSG License Manager is unavailable or available for production activation.Use local trial/offline where appropriate; prefer online activation for production when TSG License Manager is available.
Default reports vs. approved evidence pathReports need governance, retention, or central review.Set --report-dir and --history-db to approved local paths.
Manual run vs. batch/watch/scheduleOne-time processing is insufficient for the operating model.Use batch, watch, or schedule after reviewing endpoint policy.
Enable vs. restrict --no-backupA workflow requests cleanup without backups.Permit only when separate recovery controls exist.
Retain vs. purge evidenceReports, backups, or Activity data include sensitive or regulated details.Apply data governance retention and disposal policy.
Roll out vs. holdValidation evidence is incomplete or endpoint policy blocks launch.Hold deployment until package QA, installer QA, license checks, and support readiness pass.

Troubleshooting

Confidence Summary: Procedural actions in this section are 100% [verified], 0% inferred, and 0% unknown.

User-Resolvable Issues

SymptomLikely CauseAction
No source selectedNo file or folder has been chosen.Click Browse and choose a file or folder. [verified]
Scan finds nothingUnsupported file type or Recursive is not set as intended.Confirm the file type is supported and set Recursive correctly. [verified]
Reports page is emptyNo report has been generated.Use Run Cleanroom or the Cleanroom page first. [verified]
Activity is emptyNo scan, clean, or full pipeline has been recorded.Run a scan, clean, or full pipeline first. [verified]
Clean did not change filesDry Run is on, the file type is unsupported, or findings are informational.Check Dry Run, file type support, and whether findings are informational. [verified]
Verification failedResidual findings remain after cleaning.Review File Review and the post-clean report before distributing files. [verified]
Writing Assistant is missingThe package may be outdated.Ask an administrator to confirm the latest DocGuard AI package is installed. [verified]
License looks wrongThe activation state is unexpected.Use Tools -> License Status and contact an administrator. [verified]
App does not launchThe launch source or endpoint state needs review.Reopen from the extracted portable folder or contact support. [verified]

Admin-Only Issues

SymptomLikely CauseAction
Reports page is emptyNo report has been generated.Use Run Cleanroom or run sanitize run. [verified]
Activity is emptyNo run is recorded or the wrong history database is being reviewed.Check reports\history.db or pass --history-db. [verified]
License shows trialNo local or TSG license cache exists.Activate or refresh the license. [verified]
Online activation failsTSG API is unavailable or the URL is wrong.Check endpoint, firewall, DNS, and TSG_API_URL. [verified]
TSG connection is refusedTSG License Manager is not running.Start TSG License Manager on the configured port and retry. [verified]
License not foundProduct key is missing from TSG License Manager.Generate or register the license through the approved TSG process. [verified]
Invalid key formatProduct key does not match the DGA format.Use DGA-XXXX-XXXX-XXXX. [verified]
File not detectedUnsupported extension or discovery issue.Run sanitize scan <path> --diagnose. [verified]
Cleaning does not remove a findingFinding is informational, inspection-only, or requires stronger handling.Review report remediation and profile selection. [verified]
Verification failsResidual findings remain after cleaning.Review File Review and post-clean report, remediate, or document residual risk. [verified]
Portable app does not launchBlocked file, missing runtime file, endpoint policy, or endpoint protection quarantine.Re-extract the package, unblock files, check endpoint logs, and verify package hash. [verified]
Writing Assistant missingOutdated build or legacy package behavior.Deploy the latest package and confirm Extensions shows Writing Assistant as Core. [verified]

Verification Checklist

Confidence Summary: Checklist items in this section are 100% [verified], 0% inferred, and 0% unknown.

End-User Track

  • Confirm the source path is correct before scanning or cleaning. [verified]
  • Confirm you have permission to inspect and clean the selected content. [verified]
  • Confirm the selected profile matches the workflow risk level. [verified]
  • Confirm Backup is enabled unless an administrator directed another recovery path. [verified]
  • Confirm Dry Run was used before cleaning critical or unfamiliar files. [verified]
  • Confirm Scan results were reviewed in Dashboard, File Review, Findings, and Risk Map. [verified]
  • Confirm Cleanroom was used for final release or controlled document-set preparation. [verified]
  • Confirm Pipeline Verification passed or residual findings were reviewed and understood. [verified]
  • Confirm Reports were reviewed before sharing cleaned output. [verified]
  • Confirm Activity contains the expected run record. [verified]
  • Confirm reports are treated as sensitive when they include paths or finding details. [verified]

Admin Track

  • Confirm the approved installer or portable package was used. [verified]
  • Confirm endpoint users have read/execute access to the application folder. [verified]
  • Confirm endpoint users have write access to approved report and Activity locations. [verified]
  • Confirm the application title is DocGuard AI v1.2.0 RC1 U6.1. [verified]
  • Confirm Writing Assistant appears in the sidebar and Extensions lists it as Core. [verified]
  • Confirm expected Extensions are enabled with sanitize plugins. [verified]
  • Confirm license status with sanitize license or Tools -> License Status. [verified]
  • Confirm TSG activation, refresh, deactivation, and update-check paths if online licensing is used. [verified]
  • Confirm profile policy covers standard, aggressive, and paranoid. [verified]
  • Confirm --no-backup use is restricted to workflows with separate recovery controls. [verified]
  • Confirm report and Activity paths are configured with --report-dir and --history-db when defaults are unsuitable. [verified]
  • Confirm generated reports, Activity data, backups, and evidence records follow retention policy. [verified]
  • Confirm endpoint protection scans and dependency vulnerability reviews are complete before production release. [verified]
  • Confirm support handoff can collect version, installation type, Windows version, license source, workflow, source file type, profile, safety settings, report path, Activity database path, error text, and reproduction notes without full product keys or confidential document content. [verified]
